subject: Public Health
title: Gender and WASH
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Gender and WASH.

hook.text: When a village gets a water tap, the first thing that changes is girls' school attendance.

buildup.text: Women spend 200 million hours daily collecting water—time lost to education, work, and rest.

discovery.text: Lack of private toilets keeps girls home during menstruation and exposes women to violence during open defecation.

twist.text: Including women in WASH planning leads to more sustainable infrastructure—they know community needs best.

climax.text: Gender-responsive WASH programs address not just access but safety, dignity, and empowerment.

punchline.text: A water tap can free a girl to go to school.

quiz.question: How many hours do women spend daily collecting water globally?
